summary: No-ship: the committed RED proof and call-site seams are sound, but P16 promises impossible runtime logging equivalence and incorrectly claims logging cannot be tested. Simpler alternative: keep the production design unchanged and correct the contract using the existing tracing harness. Open question: are downstream logs newly reached after successful continuation explicitly part of the single flip? No tests were run.

findings.title: P16 conflates unchanged trace call sites with unchanged runtime logging
findings.body: P16 says logging behavior remains identical and waives a witness because the suite supposedly installs no tracing subscriber. However, the fix continues after a vanished entry, so later existing events—such as the quarantine warning or GC-restoration event—can be emitted where the baseline aborted first. Unchanged call sites therefore do not imply an identical log stream. The evidence premise is also false: at red.sha, pins.rs defines CaptureSubscriber (995–1037), installs it in four tests, and asserts exact event counts. Following this contract either forces suppression of legitimate downstream logs or leaves an impossible preservation claim unverified.
findings.recommendation: Narrow P16 to preserving trace call sites/schema and emitting no skip-specific event; explicitly classify downstream existing events made reachable by pass continuation as part of the allowed flip. Reuse or extend CaptureSubscriber to witness skip-path silence and expected downstream events.

next_steps: Correct P16 and its repeated logging statement at lines 1734–1745.
Add the tracing witness to B-1 acceptance before the structure gate.
